heading: Nationally significant multimodal freight and highway projects
label: 117 Nationally significant multimodal freight and highway projects

A, title I, § 11110(c)135 Stat. 475Nov. 15, 2021“(1).—In generalNotwithstanding any other provision of law, in the case of a grant described in paragraph (2), , United States Code, shall apply to the grant as if the grant was a grant provided under that section.section 117(k) of title 23“(2).—Grant describedA grant referred to in paragraph (1) is a grant that is—“(A)provided under a competitive discretionary grant program administered by the Federal Highway Administration;“(B)for a project eligible under title 23, United States Code; and“(C)in an amount greater than $5,000,000.”

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: There is established a nationally significant freight and highway projects program to provide financial assistance for projects of national or regional significance.

children.label: A
children.text: improve the safety, efficiency, and reliability of the movement of freight and people in and across rural and urban areas;

children.label: B
children.text: generate national or regional economic benefits and an increase in the global economic competitiveness of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: reduce highway or freight congestion and bottlenecks;

children.label: D
children.text: improve connectivity between modes of freight transportation;

children.label: E
children.text: enhance the resiliency of critical highway or freight infrastructure and help protect the environment;

children.label: F
children.text: improve roadways vital to national energy security, including highways that support movement of energy equipment; and

children.label: G
children.text: address the impact of population growth on the movement of people and freight.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In carrying out the program established in subsection (a), the Secretary may make grants, on a competitive basis, in accordance with this section.

children.heading: .—Grant amount
children.label: 2 .—Grant amount
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, each grant made under this section shall be in an amount that is at least $25,000,000.

children.label: A
children.text: retain not more than a total of 2 percent of the funds made available to carry out this section for the National Surface Transportation and Innovative Finance Bureau to review applications for grants under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: transfer portions of the funds retained under subparagraph (A) to the relevant Administrators to fund the award and oversight of grants provided under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: A State or a group of States.

children.label: B
children.text: A metropolitan planning organization that serves an urbanized area (as defined by the Bureau of the Census) with a population of more than 200,000 individuals.

children.label: C
children.text: A unit of local government or a group of local governments.

children.label: D
children.text: A political subdivision of a State or local government.

children.label: E
children.text: A special purpose district or public authority with a transportation function, including a port authority.

children.label: F
children.text: A Federal land management agency that applies jointly with a State or group of States.

children.label: G
children.text: A tribal government or a consortium of tribal governments.

children.label: H
children.text: A multistate corridor organization.

children.label: I
children.text: A multistate or multijurisdictional group of entities described in this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: 2 .—Applications
children.text: To be eligible for a grant under this section, an entity specified in paragraph (1) shall submit to the Secretary an application in such form, at such time, and containing such information as the Secretary determines is appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: a highway freight project carried out on the National Highway Freight Network established under section 167;

children.label: I
children.text: a project to add capacity to the Interstate System to improve mobility; or

children.label: II
children.text: a project in a national scenic area;

children.label: I
children.text: a freight intermodal or freight rail project; or

children.label: II
children.text: within the boundaries of a public or private freight rail, water (including ports), or intermodal facility and that is a surface transportation infrastructure project necessary to facilitate direct intermodal interchange, transfer, or access into or out of the facility;

children.label: iv
children.text: a railway-highway grade crossing or grade separation project;

children.label: v
children.text: a wildlife crossing project;

children.label: I
children.text: is located within the boundaries of or functionally connected to an international border crossing area in the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: improves a transportation facility owned by a Federal, State, or local government entity; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a project to add lanes;

children.label: bb
children.text: a project to add technology; and

children.label: cc
children.text: other surface transportation improvements;

children.label: I
children.text: is functionally connected to the National Highway Freight Network; and

children.label: II
children.text: is likely to reduce on-road mobile source emissions; or

children.label: viii
children.text: a highway, bridge, or freight project carried out on the National Multimodal Freight Network established under ; andsection 70103 of title 49

children.label: I
children.text: located in 1 State, 30 percent of the amount apportioned under this chapter to the State in the most recently completed fiscal year; or

children.label: II
children.text: located in more than 1 State, 50 percent of the amount apportioned under this chapter to the participating State with the largest apportionment under this chapter in the most recently completed fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: the project will make a significant improvement to freight movements on the National Highway Freight Network; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Federal share of the project funds only elements of the project that provide public benefits.

children.label: i
children.text: shall not apply to a railway-highway grade crossing or grade separation project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to a multimodal project, shall apply only to the non-highway portion or portions of the project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve not less than 15 percent of the amounts made available for grants under this section each fiscal year to make grants for projects described in subsection (d)(1)(A) that do not satisfy the minimum threshold under subsection (d)(1)(B).

children.heading: .—Grant amount
children.label: 2 .—Grant amount
children.text: Each grant made under this subsection shall be in an amount that is at least $5,000,000.

children.label: A
children.text: the cost effectiveness of the proposed project;

children.label: B
children.text: the effect of the proposed project on mobility in the State and region in which the project is carried out; and

children.label: C
children.text: the effect of the proposed project on safety on freight corridors with significant hazards, such as high winds, heavy snowfall, flooding, rockslides, mudslides, wildfire, wildlife crossing onto the roadway, or steep grades.

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 4 .—Requirement
children.text: Of the amounts reserved under paragraph (1), not less than 30 percent shall be used for projects in rural areas (as defined in subsection (i)(3)).
development phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, preliminary engineering and design work, and other preconstruction activities; and

construction, reconstruction, rehabilitation, acquisition of real property (including land related to the project and improvements to the land), environmental mitigation (including a project to replace or rehabilitate a culvert, or to reduce stormwater runoff for the purpose of improving habitat for aquatic species), construction contingencies, acquisition of equipment, and operational improvements directly related to improving system performance.
the project will generate national or regional economic, mobility, or safety benefits;
the project will be cost effective;
the project will contribute to the accomplishment of 1 or more of the national goals described under ;section 150 of this title
the project is based on the results of preliminary engineering;

children.label: A
children.text: 1 or more stable and dependable sources of funding and financing are available to construct, maintain, and operate the project; and

children.label: B
children.text: contingency amounts are available to cover unanticipated cost increases;
the project cannot be easily and efficiently completed without other Federal funding or financial assistance available to the project sponsor; and
the project is reasonably expected to begin construction not later than 18 months after the date of obligation of funds for the project.
utilization of nontraditional financing, innovative design and construction techniques, or innovative technologies;
utilization of non-Federal contributions;
contributions to geographic diversity among grant recipients, including the need for a balance between the needs of rural and urban communities;

enhancement of freight resilience to natural hazards or disasters, including high winds, heavy snowfall, flooding, rockslides, mudslides, wildfire, wildlife crossing onto the roadway, or steep grades;
whether the project will improve the shared transportation corridor of a multistate corridor organization, if applicable; and
prioritizing projects located in States in which neither the State nor an eligible entity in that State has been awarded a grant under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve not less than 25 percent of the amounts made available for grants under this section, including the amounts made available under subsection (e), each fiscal year to make grants for projects located in rural areas.

children.heading: .—Excess funding
children.label: 2 .—Excess funding
children.text: In any fiscal year in which qualified applications for grants under this subsection will not allow for the amount reserved under paragraph (1) to be fully utilized, the Secretary shall use the unutilized amounts to make grants under subsection (e).

children.heading: .—Rural area defined
children.label: 3 .—Rural area defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “rural area” means an area that is outside an urbanized area with a population of over 200,000.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B) or for a grant under subsection (q), the Federal share of the cost of a project assisted with a grant under this section may not exceed 60 percent.

children.heading: .—Small projects
children.label: B .—Small projects
children.text: In the case of a project described in subsection (e)(1), the Federal share of the cost of the project shall be 80 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: for a State with a population density of not more than 80 persons per square mile of land area, based on the 2010 census, the maximum share of the total Federal assistance provided for a project receiving a grant under this section shall be the applicable share under section 120(b); and

children.label: B
children.text: for a State not described in subparagraph (A), the total Federal assistance provided for a project receiving a grant under this section may not exceed 80 percent of the total project cost.

children.heading: .—Federal land management agencies
children.label: 3 .—Federal land management agencies
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any Federal funds other than those made available under this title or title 49 may be used to pay the non-Federal share of the cost of a project carried out under this section by a Federal land management agency, as described under subsection (c)(1)(F).

children.heading: Efficient Use of Non-Federal Funds.—
children.label: k Efficient Use of Non-Federal Funds.—
A
children.text: the grant recipient may obligate and expend non-Federal funds with respect to the project for which the grant is provided; and

children.label: B
children.text: any non-Federal funds obligated or expended in accordance with subparagraph (A) shall be credited toward the non-Federal cost share for the project for which the grant is provided.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the activities the grant recipient intends to fund;

children.label: ii
children.text: a justification for advancing the activities described in clause (i), including an assessment of the effects to the project scope, schedule, and budget if the request is not approved; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the level of risk of the activities described in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Approval
children.label: B .—Approval
children.text: The Secretary shall approve or disapprove each request submitted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Compliance with applicable requirements
children.label: C .—Compliance with applicable requirements
children.text: Any non-Federal funds obligated or expended under paragraph (1) shall comply with all applicable requirements, including any requirements included in the grant agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: affect the signing of a grant agreement or other applicable grant procedures with respect to the applicable grant;

children.label: B
children.text: create an obligation on the part of the Federal Government to repay any non-Federal funds if the grant agreement is not signed; or

children.label: C
children.text: affect the ability of the recipient of the grant to obligate or expend non-Federal funds to meet the non-Federal cost share for the project for which the grant is provided after the period described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Treatment of Freight Projects
children.label: l .—Treatment of Freight Projects
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a freight project carried out under this section shall be treated as if the project is located on a Federal-aid highway.

children.heading: .—TIFIA Program
children.label: m .—TIFIA Program
children.text: At the request of an eligible applicant under this section, the Secretary may use amounts awarded to the entity to pay subsidy and administrative costs necessary to provide the entity Federal credit assistance under chapter 6 with respect to the project for which the grant was awarded.

children.label: A
children.text: an evaluation and justification for the applicable project; and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the amount of the proposed grant award.

children.heading: .—Congressional disapproval
children.label: 2 .—Congressional disapproval
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant or any other obligation or commitment to fund a project under this section if a joint resolution is enacted disapproving funding for the project before the last day of the 60-day period described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which a grant recipient for a project under this section is selected, the Secretary shall provide to each eligible applicant not selected for that grant a written notification that the eligible applicant was not selected.

children.label: A
children.text: detail on the evaluation of the application of the eligible applicant; and

children.label: B
children.text: an explanation of and guidance on the reasons the application was not selected for a grant under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the eligible applicant receives a written notification under paragraph (1), if the eligible applicant opts to receive a debrief described in paragraph (2), the eligible applicant shall notify the Secretary that the eligible applicant is requesting a debrief.

children.heading: .—Debrief
children.label: B .—Debrief
children.text: If the eligible applicant submits a request for a debrief under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall provide the debrief by not later than 60 days after the date on which the Secretary receives the request for a debrief.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, not later than 30 days after the date on which the Secretary selects a project for funding under this section, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report that describes the reasons for selecting the project, based on any criteria established by the Secretary in accordance with this section.

children.heading: .—Inclusions
children.label: B .—Inclusions
children.text: The report submitted under subparagraph (A) shall specify each criterion established by the Secretary that the project meets.

children.heading: .—Availability
children.label: C .—Availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make available on the website of the Department of Transportation the report submitted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Applicability
children.label: D .—Applicability
children.text: This paragraph applies to all projects described in subparagraph (A) that the Secretary selects on or after .October 1, 2021

children.heading: .—Assessment
children.label: A .—Assessment
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct an assessment of the establishment, solicitation, selection, and justification process with respect to the funding of projects under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the process by which each project was selected;

children.label: ii
children.text: the factors that went into the selection of each project; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the justification for the selection of each project based on any criteria established by the Secretary in accordance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct an assessment of the establishment, solicitation, selection, and justification process with respect to the funding of projects under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a final report that describes the findings of the Inspector General of the Department of Transportation with respect to the assessment conducted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: 1 .—Establishment
children.text: There is established a pilot program to award grants to eligible applicants for projects eligible for grants under this section (referred to in this subsection as the “pilot program”).

children.heading: .—Priority
children.label: 2 .—Priority
children.text: In awarding grants under the pilot program, the Secretary shall give priority to an application that offers a greater non-Federal share of the cost of a project relative to other applications under the pilot program.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Federal share of the cost of a project assisted with a grant under the pilot program may not exceed 50 percent.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: For grants awarded under the pilot program, except as provided in clause (ii), an eligible applicant may not use Federal assistance to satisfy the non-Federal share of the cost under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: ii .—Exception
children.text: An eligible applicant may use funds from a secured loan (as defined in section 601(a)) to satisfy the non-Federal share of the cost under subparagraph (A) if the loan is repayable from non-Federal funds.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Of the amounts made available to provide grants under this section, the Secretary shall reserve for each fiscal year $150,000,000 to provide grants under the pilot program.

children.heading: .—Unutilized amounts
children.label: B .—Unutilized amounts
children.text: In any fiscal year during which applications under this subsection are insufficient to effect an award or allocation of the entire amount reserved under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall use the unutilized amounts to provide other grants under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Of the amounts reserved under paragraph (4)(A), the Secretary shall reserve for each fiscal year not less than 10 percent for projects eligible for a grant under subsection (e).

children.label: I
children.text: the requirements of subsection (e) shall apply; and

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements of subsection (g) shall not apply.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Of the amounts reserved under paragraph (4)(A), the Secretary shall reserve for each fiscal year not less than 25 percent for projects eligible for a grant under subsection (i).

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: ii .—Requirement
children.text: For a grant awarded from the amount reserved under clause (i), the requirements of subsection (i) shall apply.

children.label: A
children.text: the number, types, and locations of eligible applicants that have applied for grants under the pilot program;

children.label: B
children.text: the number, types, and locations of grant recipients under the pilot program;

children.label: C
children.text: an assessment of whether implementation of the pilot program has incentivized eligible applicants to offer a greater non-Federal share for grants under the pilot program; and

children.label: D
children.text: any recommendations for modifications to the pilot program.

children.heading: .—Multistate Corridor Organization Defined
children.label: r .—Multistate Corridor Organization Defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “multistate corridor organization” means an organization of a group of States developed through cooperative agreements, coalitions, or other arrangements to promote regional cooperation, planning, and shared project implementation for programs and projects to improve transportation system management and operations for a shared transportation corridor.
$1,000,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;
$1,100,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;
$1,200,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;
$1,300,000,000 for fiscal year 2025; and
$1,400,000,000 for fiscal year 2026.
